FAQ.TITLE: Frequently Asked Questions
FAQ.Q1: What is the UAE Corporate Tax rate?
FAQ.A1: The standard corporate tax rate is 9% on taxable income exceeding AED 375,000. Under this threshold, the rate is 0%.
FAQ.Q2: How often is my bookkeeping updated?
FAQ.A2: We reconcile transactions daily using our AI engine, giving you a real-time view of cash, profit, and tax liability.
FAQ.Q3: Do I get a dedicated accountant?
FAQ.A3: Yes. Every client is assigned a dedicated, Chartered Accountant (Big 4–trained) who reviews and signs off on all reports.
FAQ.Q4: Can you help with FTA audits?
FAQ.A4: Absolutely. We provide full representation and audit defense support in case of FTA inquiries or audits.

HOME.STAKES_TITLE:
Every Month You Wait,
The Risk Compounds.
HOME.STAKES_SUBTITLE: These are not warnings. These are documented outcomes UAE businesses face every year — all 100% preventable with the right financial partner in place from day one.
HOME.STAKES_C1_TITLE: FTA Penalties That Cost More Than Prevention
HOME.STAKES_C1_DESC: Late VAT filings carry FTA penalties from AED 1,000 per period, rising to AED 2,000 for repeat violations. One missed deadline costs more than a full year of professional accounting fees — and leaves a compliance record that follows your business.
HOME.STAKES_C2_TITLE: Lost Funding at the Final Stage
HOME.STAKES_C2_DESC: Banks and investors require clean, IFRS-compliant financials before approving loans or equity rounds. Disorganised books don't just delay opportunities — they destroy them at the final stage, when it matters most.
HOME.STAKES_C3_TITLE: Cash Flow Crisis — Invisible Until Too Late
HOME.STAKES_C3_DESC: Without real-time cash flow management and financial forecasting, profitable businesses run out of money. Not because the business failed — but because nobody was monitoring the numbers with precision and alerting you before the problem compounded.

HOME.LEAN_DEMO_Q1: Does our holding company qualify for 0% Corporate Tax under Qualifying Free Zone rules?
HOME.LEAN_DEMO_A1: ✅ Yes — subject to conditions. Under UAE CT Law Article 18, QFZP status requires: (1) adequate substance in the Freezone, (2) qualifying income only, (3) non-qualifying revenue ≤5% or AED 5M. Want me to run a full QFZP eligibility check?
HOME.LEAN_DEMO_A2: 📄 Processed. Al Fardan Trading · AED 18,450 · VAT AED 922.50 · TRN ✅ Verified · Category: Office Supplies. Reconciled & archived. ⚠️ Invoice date outside VAT period — flagged.
HOME.LEAN_DEMO_INPUT: Ask tax law, VAT, upload doc…
HOME.LEAN_DEMO_POWERED_BY: Powered by GPT-4o · Claude 3 · Gemini · UAE PDPL Compliant 🔒

HOME.FAQ_TALK_EXPERT: Talk to a Real Expert — Free
HOME.FAQ_TALK_EXPERT_DESC: Book a free 30-minute call with one of our Chartered Accountants. No sales pitch — just honest advice for your business.
HOME.FAQ_Q1: Are you licensed to handle UAE Corporate Tax?
HOME.FAQ_A1: Yes. Booklean's team includes Chartered Accountants (ICAI, ACCA, CPA qualified) with direct experience in UAE Corporate Tax (9%), VAT, and FTA compliance. We handle Corporate Tax registration, structuring, filing, and audit representation. Our AI platform is trained specifically on UAE VAT and CT law and is supervised by qualified tax experts — not generic tools.
HOME.FAQ_Q2: How is this different from just using accounting software?
HOME.FAQ_A2: Software processes transactions — it doesn't think, advise, or take responsibility. Booklean gives you the technology (AI bookkeeping, dashboards, automated reconciliation) plus a team of Chartered Accountants who review every report, handle your FTA filings, and call you when something looks off. Think of us as your full finance department — not a tool you manage.
HOME.FAQ_Q3: Can you work alongside my existing free zone auditor?
HOME.FAQ_A3: Absolutely. Many of our clients have a free zone registered auditor for statutory requirements, and we handle their day-to-day bookkeeping, VAT, Corporate Tax, and CFO advisory. We deliver clean, audit-ready books that make your annual audit faster and cheaper. We can also take over the full statutory audit if you'd like to consolidate.
HOME.FAQ_Q4: How long before my books are fully cleaned up?
HOME.FAQ_A4: For most businesses, the initial cleanup and system setup takes 2–4 weeks depending on how much history needs to be corrected. After that, your books stay clean automatically — no backlogs, no catching up. We'll give you a specific timeline after your free discovery call once we've assessed your current situation.
HOME.FAQ_Q5: Do you handle 0% Corporate Tax for Qualifying Free Zone companies?
HOME.FAQ_A5: Yes. Free Zone companies may qualify for 0% Corporate Tax as a Qualifying Free Zone Person (QFZP) under UAE CT Law Article 18. We assess your eligibility, ensure substance requirements are met, and handle registration and filing. If you don't qualify for 0%, we optimise your position under the 9% rate. We also handle mixed-activity entities where some income qualifies and some doesn't.
HOME.FAQ_Q6: Is my financial data safe with Booklean?
HOME.FAQ_A6: Completely. All data is stored in UAE-based servers (Azure/AWS UAE), encrypted with AES-256, and fully compliant with UAE PDPL regulations. We operate a zero-knowledge architecture — your data is yours. We never share, sell, or retain it beyond our service agreement.

FAQ_TITLE:
Common UAE Tax &
Finance Questions. Answered Honestly.
FAQ_SUBTITLE: Written by Big 4–trained Chartered Accountants. No jargon. No sales language. The exact information UAE business owners need to make confident financial decisions.
FAQ_TALK_EXPERT: Talk to a Real Expert — Free
FAQ_TALK_EXPERT_DESC: Our CAs answer your specific UAE tax, VAT, or compliance question — free, no obligation, within 24 hours.
FAQ_FACT_2: 🟢 VAT standard rate: 5% on most goods & services
FAQ_FACT_3: 🟢 Late VAT filing penalty: From AED 1,000 per period
FAQ_FACT_5: 🟢 VAT registration threshold: AED 375,000 annual turnover
DIFF_BTN_CALL: Get My Free UAE Compliance Assessment →
FAQ_Q1: What is UAE Corporate Tax (9%) and who must pay it?
FAQ_A1: UAE Corporate Tax is a 9% federal tax on business net taxable income exceeding AED 375,000 per year, under Federal Decree-Law No. 47 of 2022. Businesses earning below AED 375,000 are taxed at 0%. It applies to all UAE mainland companies and partnerships. Free zone companies may qualify for 0% as Qualifying Free Zone Persons (QFZP) subject to FTA substance requirements. BookLean manages complete CT registration on EmaraTax, annual return filing, and ongoing tax planning — so you always pay exactly what is right, and not a dirham more.
FAQ_Q2: What are the UAE FTA penalties for late VAT or Corporate Tax filing?
FAQ_A2: Late VAT filing penalties start at AED 1,000 per period for the first offence and rise to AED 2,000 for repeat violations. Late VAT payment triggers an immediate 2% penalty plus 4% per month on the unpaid amount. Late Corporate Tax return filing attracts AED 500 per month for the first year, then AED 1,000 per month thereafter. Late VAT registration carries penalties from AED 10,000 to AED 20,000. BookLean guarantees zero FTA penalties across all client accounts — one missed deadline costs more than a full year of our fees.
FAQ_Q3: Do free zone companies pay UAE Corporate Tax? What is QFZP status?
FAQ_A3: Free zone companies can qualify as Qualifying Free Zone Persons (QFZP) and enjoy 0% Corporate Tax on qualifying income. This requires meeting FTA economic substance requirements, earning income only from qualifying activities, and maintaining arm's-length transactions with mainland entities. Non-qualifying income, such as revenue from UAE mainland clients, is still taxed at 9%. BookLean assesses your IFZA, DMCC, Meydan, or other free zone structure, confirms your QFZP eligibility, and maximises every legal exemption while keeping you fully FTA-compliant.
FAQ_Q4: What is PEPPOL e-invoicing in UAE and when is it mandatory?
FAQ_A4: PEPPOL e-invoicing is the UAE FTA's mandatory digital invoicing standard requiring VAT-registered businesses to issue and receive tax invoices in structured XML format through a certified PEPPOL Access Point, replacing paper and PDF invoices for B2B transactions. The FTA is rolling out the mandate in phases from 2026. Non-compliance will attract FTA penalties. BookLean delivers end-to-end PEPPOL implementation: Access Point selection, ERP integration (Zoho Books, QuickBooks, Xero, SAP), XML configuration, and ongoing FTA compliance monitoring so you meet every phase deadline.
FAQ_Q5: What is the UAE VAT registration threshold and who needs to register?
FAQ_A5: Any UAE business with taxable supplies or imports exceeding AED 375,000 in the previous 12 months, or expected to exceed this in the next 30 days, must register for VAT with the FTA. Voluntary registration is available from AED 187,500. Registration is done through the FTA EmaraTax portal. Once registered, you must charge 5% VAT on standard-rated supplies, file quarterly or monthly VAT returns, and maintain FTA-compliant tax invoices. BookLean handles the full registration process, TRN issuance, and ongoing compliance so your business is protected from day one.
FAQ_Q6: Mainland vs Freezone UAE — which is better, and how long does company setup take?
FAQ_A6: The right choice depends on your business model and customers. Mainland companies can trade freely across the UAE and government contracts with 100% foreign ownership now permitted in most sectors, and setup typically takes 5–10 working days. Free zone companies offer 0% Corporate Tax on qualifying income, 100% repatriation of profits, and faster setup (3–7 days), but have restrictions on direct mainland trading. Key decision factors: where are your clients, whether you need a physical UAE presence, and whether you qualify for QFZP status. BookLean provides a free structure assessment to identify the most tax-efficient and commercially practical option for your specific business.
FAQ_Q7: What is ESR filing in UAE and who needs to file?
FAQ_A7: Economic Substance Regulations (ESR) require UAE businesses in certain sectors, including banking, insurance, investment fund management, intellectual property, shipping, holding companies, headquarters, and distribution, to demonstrate genuine economic activity in the UAE. All in-scope entities must file an annual ESR Notification and, if conducting a Relevant Activity, submit an ESR Report within 12 months of the financial year-end. Penalties for non-filing start at AED 50,000. BookLean assesses your ESR obligations, prepares and submits both filings on time, and maintains the substance documentation needed to pass any FTA review.
FAQ_Q8: Does BookLean provide Virtual CFO services in UAE? What does a vCFO do?
FAQ_A8: Yes. BookLean's Virtual CFO service gives your business Big 4-level financial leadership at a fraction of the cost of a full-time hire. Your dedicated vCFO delivers monthly management accounts (P&L, balance sheet, cash flow), KPI dashboards, 12-month cash flow forecasting, budgeting, scenario planning, and board-ready investor presentations. They also oversee FTA compliance, banking relationships, and cost optimisation. This service is ideal for SMEs, startups, and fast-growing businesses in Dubai and across the UAE that need strategic financial direction without a AED 40,000+ monthly salary commitment.
